evals.prompt: I just finished implementing the user authentication module for the web app. Wrote JWT token generation, login/signup endpoints, and password hashing with bcrypt. All 23 tests pass. I should record this somewhere so I can pick it up later.
evals.expected_output: A daily journal entry created documenting the authentication module implementation with details about what was built, verification results, and decisions made.

assertions.name: follows-template-structure
assertions.description: Journal entry uses the standard template sections: ## Summary, ## Key Decisions, ## Verification, ## Lessons Learned

assertions.name: includes-auth-details
assertions.description: Entry contains details about JWT, login/signup endpoints, and bcrypt

assertions.name: correct-daily-filename
assertions.description: Entry is saved with YYYY-MM-DD.md filename format (not including agent name suffix)

assertions.name: saved-in-daily-path
assertions.description: Entry is saved in the daily/ subdirectory of the journal path

assertions.name: includes-verification
assertions.description: Entry includes verification results (23 tests pass)

evals.prompt: Hey, I just started this session. I need to check if there's anything I was working on before so I know what to pick up. Can you look up my recent context?
evals.expected_output: The most recent daily journal entry read and its contents summarized or displayed for context recovery.

assertions.name: reads-from-daily-directory
assertions.description: Agent reads journal files from the daily/ subdirectory

assertions.name: uses-glob-or-ls-for-discovery
assertions.description: Agent uses glob or ls to discover journal files (not assuming a fixed filename)

assertions.name: identifies-most-recent-entry
assertions.description: Agent identifies the most recent journal entry

assertions.name: summarizes-latest-work
assertions.description: Agent correctly summarizes the latest task from the most recent entry

evals.prompt: I'm a research agent working on a project about distributed systems. Last week I was reading about consensus algorithms and I had some notes about Raft vs Paxos. I need to find those notes again to continue my report.
evals.expected_output: Journal entries related to distributed systems / consensus algorithms research found and relevant information extracted.

assertions.name: reads-multiple-entries
assertions.description: Agent reads multiple daily journal entries to find the relevant information

assertions.name: finds-raft-paxos-content
assertions.description: Agent successfully identifies the Raft vs Paxos comparison notes

assertions.name: extracts-prototype-details
assertions.description: Agent finds and reports the Go prototype details from follow-up entries

assertions.name: uses-structured-search
assertions.description: Agent follows a structured approach (checks journals, reads entries) rather than guessing

evals.prompt: It's Sunday and I need to consolidate all my daily journal entries from this week into a weekly summary. My name is 'data-pipeline-agent' and I've been working on ETL pipeline improvements all week.
evals.expected_output: A weekly consolidation journal entry created that summarizes the week's daily entries into a brief overview.

assertions.name: uses-consolidation-script
assertions.description: Agent runs the provided consolidation script to find source files

assertions.name: covers-all-days
assertions.description: Weekly summary covers all daily entries from the week

assertions.name: uses-iso-week-format
assertions.description: Summary saved with YYYY-Wnn.md naming convention

assertions.name: shorter-than-sum-of-dailies
assertions.description: Consolidated summary is more concise than the combined daily entries

assertions.name: identifies-patterns
assertions.description: Summary identifies cross-day patterns or themes

evals.prompt: This is my very first session ever. I'm a new agent called 'code-reviewer-v2' and I need to set up my journal so I can start tracking my work properly.
evals.expected_output: Initial journal entry created and/or journal directory structure explained with guidance on how to proceed.

assertions.name: creates-full-directory-structure
assertions.description: Agent creates the complete journal directory hierarchy (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly)

assertions.name: uses-standard-journal-path
assertions.description: Agent creates journals at ~/agent-notes/<AGENT_NAME>/journals/

assertions.name: writes-first-entry
assertions.description: Agent writes an initial daily journal entry

assertions.name: follows-entry-template
assertions.description: First journal entry follows the skill's template structure

assertions.name: correct-date-filename
assertions.description: Entry uses YYYY-MM-DD.md without agent name suffix
